meta.title: Lev Effect Boundary Audit
meta.description: Current tangle versus ideal effect-centered Lev runtime boundaries, with module duplication map and C1-C4 diagrams.

props.title: Lev Effect Boundary Audit
props.subtitle: Current tangle vs. effect-centered runtime boundaries
props.meta: Generated 2026-06-03 from live repo scan + web search

props.content: The current code already contains the right primitives, but they are split across `domain`, `orchestration`, `exec`, `flowmind`, receipt, proof, eval, and run-fabric surfaces. The clean direction is not a new taxonomy; it is consolidation around `EffectEnvelope -> ACT through exec -> ObservedEffect -> evaluator verdicts -> sealed typed receipt`.

props.label: Read First
props.title: The Architectural Answer
props.subtitle: The naming problem is real, but the deeper problem is ownership.

props.title: Current
props.content: `core/orchestration` is doing graph traversal, queues, topology, proof, receipt, effect declarations, effect-control, eval result projection, CDO handlers, worktree utilities, and direct shell execution. That is too many reasons to change in one package.

props.title: Ideal
props.content: Keep agent orchestration for multi-agent coordination. Build Lev runtime from bottom-up primitives: shared domain contracts, FlowMind declarations, exec as the only effectful path, an effect controller/evaluator engine, a run ledger, and projections for eval/proof/receipts.

props.label: orchestration shell/process sites called out by workstream
Web Search
props.title: Why `agent orchestration` is a loaded term now
props.subtitle: Industry usage has converged on multi-agent coordination, not Lev's low-level runtime algebra.

rows.current_usage: Coordinating multiple specialized AI agents in one system to achieve shared objectives.
rows.architecture_pressure: The word implies agent-to-agent coordination, role assignment, dependency management, and shared workflow control.

rows.current_usage: Multiagent orchestration patterns: sequential, concurrent, group chat, handoff, magentic; adds overhead, latency, and failure modes.
rows.architecture_pressure: Use orchestration only when the system is actually coordinating agents; do not hide primitive runtime concerns there.

rows.current_usage: Governance and execution layer coordinating multiple autonomous agents under constraints, state, policy, HITL, and audit.
rows.architecture_pressure: A package named orchestration should read like multi-agent control, not an effect/proof/eval/run dumping ground.

props.title: Naming implication
props.content: If Lev keeps a package called `core/orchestration`, it should own agent coordination patterns: team handoffs, group-chat/tribunal style flows, multi-agent task allocation, shared state, and governance. It should not own primitive effect sealing, proof refs, typed receipts, direct shell execution, or eval scorecards.

props.label: Current C1-C4
props.title: Current architecture: useful pieces, tangled ownership
props.subtitle: The current package graph has multiple ways to say effect, verdict, receipt, run, proof, and execution.

props.caption: C1 current: Lev-facing consumers enter through several surfaces that converge late and inconsistently.
props.content:
flowchart LR
  U["User / agent / automation"] --> Poly["core/poly surfaces"]
  U --> Flow["FlowMind authoring"]
  U --> Exec["lev exec"]
  U --> PM[".lev/pm workstreams"]
  Poly --> Orch["core/orchestration"]
  Flow --> Orch
  Exec --> ExecCore["core/exec"]
  PM --> Proof["proof / receipt artifacts"]
  Orch --> Proof
  Orch --> DirectShell["direct shell/process sites"]
  Orch --> LocalEval["local EvalResult + EffectResult"]
  ExecCore --> RunTypes["exec run/proof refs"]
  Proof --> Claim["claimed certification"]
  DirectShell -. "bypasses blessed path" .-> Claim

props.caption: C2 current: containers by package show overlap, not clean hex boundaries.
props.content:
flowchart TB
  subgraph Domain["core/domain"]
    EC["effect-control.ts\nEffectEnvelope / ObservedEffect / EffectReceipt"]
    RF["run-fabric.ts\nRunEnvelope / typed refs / canCertifyExecution"]
    EP["execution-protocol.ts\nExecutionRunRequest / Response"]
  end
  subgraph Orchestration["core/orchestration"]
    ECG["effect-control/*\npolicy / evaluators / receipt / shell gateway"]
    ETypes["effect/types.ts\nEffectDeclaration / EffectResult"]
    EvalTypes["eval/types.ts\nEvalResult / EvalStrategy"]
    Receipts["receipt/*\nReceipt embeds eval + effect results"]
    OFabric["run-fabric/*\nRunService / JSONL leases"]
    Loop["loop + execution-contract + topology + queue"]
    Shell["child_process shell sites"]
  end
  subgraph Exec["core/exec"]
    ExecRun["run/types.ts\nExecutionRunEnvelope + rich EvidenceRef"]
    ExecEval["eval/*\nEvalRegistry / scorecards"]
    ExecCmd["exec-command + transport\nblessed execution bridge"]
  end
  subgraph FlowMind["core/flowmind"]
    FMEC["effect-control-runtime.ts\ncallback shell"]
    GateEval["gate-evaluator.ts\nGateEvalResult"]
    RunPort["run-port + node-run-adapter\nseparate exec bridge"]
  end
  EC --> ECG
  ECG --> Receipts
  ECG --> EvalTypes
  ECG --> ETypes
  RF --> OFabric
  EP --> ExecCmd
  ExecRun --> ExecEval
  FMEC -. callbacks .-> ECG
  Shell -. duplicates .-> ExecCmd
  RunPort -. duplicates .-> ExecCmd

props.caption: C3 current: the orchestration package is a hive of unrelated component owners.
props.content:
flowchart LR
  Orch["@lev-os/orchestration"] --> A2A["a2a/dispatcher.ts\nagent messaging"]
  Orch --> EC["execution-contract/core.ts + adapter.ts\ngraph walk + fulfill"]
  Orch --> Loop["loop/*\nheartbeat / tick / until / iterative runner"]
  Orch --> Topo["topology/*\nDAG + scheduler"]
  Orch --> Queue["queue/*\ndurable task queues"]
  Orch --> Effect["effect/types.ts\nlocal effect primitive"]
  Orch --> EffectControl["effect-control/*\nevaluator/seal engine"]
  Orch --> Eval["eval/types.ts\nEvalResult"]
  Orch --> Receipt["receipt/*\nReceipt factory/schema"]
  Orch --> Fabric["run-fabric/*\nrun/action ledger"]
  Orch --> Proof["proof/*\nintent conservation / proof spec generator"]
  Orch --> CDO["handlers/cdo.ts\nClaude shell handler"]
  Orch --> Git["git/worktree.ts\nworktree utility"]
  EffectControl --> ShellGateway["shell-gateway.ts + shell-command.ts\ndirect spawn"]
  Proof --> ProofShell["proof-spec-generator.ts\nexecSync checks"]
  CDO --> CdoShell["execSync claude -p"]
  Topo --> BdShell["dag.ts execSync bd"]

props.caption: C4 current: duplicated code-level concepts that should converge.
props.content:
flowchart TB
  Verdicts["Verdicts"] --> V1["domain/effect-control.ts\nEffectVerdict: pass/fail/unclear"]
  Verdicts --> V2["orchestration/eval/types.ts\nEvalResult.passed boolean"]
  Verdicts --> V3["flowmind/gate-evaluator.ts\nGateEvalResult"]
  Verdicts --> V4["exec/run/types.ts\nGateProofRef.verdict/status"]
  Refs["Proof refs"] --> R1["EffectReceipt string[] refs"]
  Refs --> R2["domain/run-fabric typed refs"]
  Refs --> R3["exec/run/types EvidenceRef hierarchy"]
  Effects["Effects"] --> E1["domain EffectEnvelope / ObservedEffect"]
  Effects --> E2["orchestration EffectDeclaration / EffectResult"]
  Effects --> E3["receipt.effectResults projection"]
  Runs["Runs"] --> Run1["domain RunEnvelope"]
  Runs --> Run2["exec ExecutionRunEnvelope"]
  Runs --> Run3["orchestration RunService ledger"]
  Execution["Execution"] --> X1["exec-command / transport"]
  Execution --> X2["orchestration shell-command.ts"]
  Execution --> X3["flowmind node-run-adapter.ts"]
  Execution --> X4["harness process runners"]

props.label: Ideal C1-C4
props.title: Ideal architecture: effect-centered and hexagonal
props.subtitle: One substrate, multiple lenses: semantic control, effect policy, user evals, and proof gates all normalize onto the same evaluator/effect path.

props.caption: C1 ideal: users see workflows/evals/proofs; internally Lev runs one effect/evaluator substrate.
props.content:
flowchart LR
  U["User / agent / automation"] --> Author["FlowMind / skills / eval suites"]
  Author --> Runtime["Lev runtime primitives"]
  Runtime --> Exec["Exec: only effectful bridge"]
  Exec --> Evidence["traces / receipts / GateProof refs"]
  Evidence --> Projection["eval reports / semantic-control decisions / dashboards"]
  Projection --> U
  Runtime --> AgentOrch["Agent orchestration\nonly when multi-agent coordination is real"]

props.caption: C2 ideal: containers separate contracts, authoring, execution, effect control, run ledger, eval projection, and agent orchestration.
props.content:
flowchart TB
  Domain["core/domain\nshared contracts only"] --> Flow["core/flowmind\nauthoring + graph declarations"]
  Domain --> Effect["core/effect or renamed effect-control package\nadmit / evaluate / seal"]
  Domain --> Exec["core/exec\nExecutionRunRequest / Response + proof runner"]
  Domain --> Ledger["run ledger\nrun/action leases + replay state"]
  Flow --> Traversal["graph/loop traversal package\nnode/edge/gate/loop program runner"]
  Traversal --> Effect
  Effect --> Exec
  Exec --> Harness["harness / tmux-harness\nprocess/session adapters"]
  Effect --> Ledger
  Exec --> Ledger
  Effect --> Eval["core/eval projection\nsuites / cases / scorecards"]
  Ledger --> Eval
  AgentOrch["agent orchestration package\nteam / handoff / group chat / multi-agent governance"] --> Flow
  AgentOrch --> Traversal

props.caption: C3 ideal: effect controller owns control semantics; exec owns execution; projections do not become engines.
props.content:
flowchart LR
  subgraph EffectController["Effect controller"]
    Admit["admit envelope\npolicy + authority"] --> ActPort["call exec port"]
    ActPort --> Observe["normalize ObservedEffect"]
    Observe --> Evaluate["run evaluator programs"]
    Evaluate --> Seal["seal EffectReceipt"]
  end
  subgraph ExecCore["Exec"]
    Request["ExecutionRunRequest"] --> Blessed["tmux / harness / tool transport"]
    Blessed --> Response["ExecutionRunResponse + typed evidence refs"]
  end
  subgraph Projections["Projections"]
    EvalProj["EvalRun / scorecard"]
    SemProj["semantic-control decision"]
    ReceiptProj["user receipt / dashboard"]
  end
  ActPort --> Request
  Response --> Observe
  Seal --> EvalProj
  Seal --> SemProj
  Seal --> ReceiptProj

props.caption: C4 ideal: one execution/effect loop; no private evaluator exec type needed.
props.content:
sequenceDiagram
  participant Flow as FlowMind / eval suite / core gate
  participant Eff as EffectController
  participant Exec as core/exec
  participant H as tmux or harness adapter
  participant Store as run ledger + proof refs
  Flow->>Eff: EffectEnvelope + evaluator bindings + vars
  Eff->>Eff: admit(policy, authority, scope)
  Eff->>Exec: ExecutionRunRequest(parent_effect_id)
  Exec->>H: execute through blessed adapter
  H-->>Exec: process/session/tool result
  Exec-->>Eff: ExecutionRunResponse + typed evidence refs
  Eff->>Eff: build ObservedEffect
  Eff->>Eff: run evaluator programs
  Eff->>Store: EffectReceipt + TraceRef + GateProofRef lineage
  Store-->>Flow: verdict + reasons + route

props.label: Module Breakdown
props.title: What exists where, and what it duplicates
props.subtitle: File-level inventory from the live tree.

rows.duplicates: Receipt refs are string arrays while run-fabric and exec have typed refs.
rows.keep: This is the best current effect vocabulary.
rows.target: Stay in domain for shared contracts; move typed refs into it or import shared typed refs.

rows.duplicates: Overlaps with exec `ExecutionRunEnvelope`; proof refs compete with exec run refs.
rows.keep: Typed refs and fail-closed certification rule.
rows.target: Split into shared typed refs + operational run ledger contracts.

rows.keep: Canonical port between effect/evaluator work and exec.
rows.target: Stay in domain as port contract.

rows.current: Actual engine today: policy admission, observed-effect extraction, evaluator verdicts, sealing, receipt projection, shell gateway.
rows.duplicates: Owns effect engine but lives beside queues/topology/proof and includes direct shell execution.
rows.target: Move/consolidate into effect controller package; remove direct shell gateway or route it through exec.
Fold into domain/effect semantics or retire as projection.

rows.duplicates: Competes with effect verdicts and exec eval scorecards.
rows.keep: Append-only result concept if projection remains useful.
rows.target: Eval projection layer, not effect engine.

rows.duplicates: Projects domain `EffectReceipt` into a second receipt shape at seal time.
rows.keep: User-facing receipt shape if still valuable.
rows.target: Projection over sealed effect receipts, not primary proof source.

rows.current: RunService, JSONL store, idempotent run/action ledger, claim/heartbeat/complete/fail.
rows.duplicates: Operational run ledger is mixed with proof/effect semantics.
rows.keep: Idempotency, action leases, replayable JSONL run store.
rows.target: Run ledger package; no proof ontology ownership.

rows.duplicates: Another run/ref model beside domain run-fabric and EffectReceipt strings.
rows.keep: Rich typed evidence hierarchy and proof bundle assembly.
rows.target: Exec-owned run envelope with shared domain ref primitives.

rows.current: Eval registry, scorecards, benchmark/AI-SRE/sandbox eval validators.
rows.duplicates: Eval suite language exists but not unified with effect-control evaluator engine.
rows.target: Eval framework projection over effect/evaluator substrate.

rows.current: Callback shell for admission, ACT, verify, seal; imports domain types only.
rows.duplicates: Looks like engine but delegates all real behavior to injected callbacks.
rows.target: FlowMind declaration shell that calls the canonical effect controller.

rows.duplicates: Gate verdict/result language separate from effect verdicts and eval results.
rows.target: Evaluator binding over normalized effect/eval verdict payloads.

rows.current: Graph start/next traversal and adapter fulfillment for agent/function/gate/loop kinds.
rows.duplicates: Execution adapter shell overlaps with exec and FlowMind run port.
rows.keep: Pure traversal state machine if cleaned.
rows.target: Rename/split as graph or loop runtime; not agent orchestration.

rows.current: Intent conservation proof, proof spec generation, receipt policy, witness accumulation.
rows.duplicates: Proof refs and eval conversion overlap with domain semantic-control and exec proof bundles.
rows.target: Effect/evaluator proof projection; remove shell proof checks from orchestration.

rows.duplicates: Other packages spawn shells directly instead of using this path.
rows.target: Only execution adapters allowed to touch tmux/process mechanics.

props.label: Duplication
props.title: Duplicate concept map
props.subtitle: These are the concrete split-brain seams the roadmap should collapse first.

rows.collapse_rule: Evaluator emits normalized verdict + reasons; projections adapt display vocabulary.

rows.current_shapes: EffectReceipt string arrays, domain run-fabric typed refs, exec EvidenceRef hierarchy.
rows.problem: A receipt can look proof-bearing without typed lineage.
rows.collapse_rule: Typed refs become shared primitives; string refs only for legacy projections.

rows.problem: Effect ontology and receipt projection are conflated.
rows.collapse_rule: Envelope/observed/receipt is source; declaration/result is invariant/projection if needed.

rows.problem: Operational ledger and proof-bearing execution envelope blur.
rows.collapse_rule: Ledger tracks attempts/leases; exec envelope tracks execution identity/evidence.

rows.current_shapes: exec transport, orchestration shell gateway, FlowMind node-run-adapter, harness process runners.
rows.problem: Multiple mutation paths make effect proof unverifiable.
rows.collapse_rule: Only exec/harness/tmux performs effects; every other package calls the port.

rows.current_shapes: Effect evaluator verdicts, orchestration EvalResult, exec EvalRegistry/scorecards, FlowMind gates.
rows.problem: Eval framework risks becoming another engine.
rows.collapse_rule: Eval suites are bindings/cases/scorers over the effect/evaluator substrate.

props.label: Run Fabric
props.title: What to steal from run-fabric without keeping the split brain
props.subtitle: Effect was supposed to supersede run-fabric as proof language; run-fabric still contains useful operational machinery.

columns.label: Risk if left where it is

rows.value: Typed proof lineage, better than string arrays.
rows.risk: EffectReceipt stays weak and receipt theatre remains possible.
rows.move: Promote typed refs to shared domain proof/evidence refs.

rows.value: Fail-closed: certification requires GateProof + ReceiptRef + TraceRef.
rows.risk: Certification policy lives in run-fabric instead of the effect/evaluator seal.
rows.move: Keep rule; invoke it during effect receipt sealing/proof projection.

rows.risk: Looks like it owns proof semantics because it builds envelopes.
rows.move: Keep as run ledger/application service; strip proof ontology to refs/status only.

rows.value: Richer proof bundle inputs and replay support.
rows.risk: Competes with run-fabric typed refs and domain effect evidence refs.
rows.move: Unify with shared typed evidence refs; exec can extend with adapter-specific metadata.

props.title: Run-fabric decomposition
props.content: The ledger concern is real: idempotent runs, action leases, heartbeats, retry state, replay indexes. The proof concern should move under effect/evaluator receipt sealing. That means run-fabric becomes an operational log, not the semantic proof fabric.

props.label: DDD / Hex
props.title: Bottom-up buckets from first principles
props.subtitle: Start with reasons to change, not with existing package names.

rows.owns: Shared value objects and schemas used by more than one core consumer.
FlowMind graph documents, nodes, edges, gates, evaluator bindings, config knobs.
Given a graph state, choose next node/edge/gate/loop transition.
rows.must_not_own: Agent orchestration strategy, direct shell, proof ontology.
rows.candidate_home: Rename/split from core/orchestration into graph-loop/runtime traversal

rows.owns: Admission, ACT port call, observed effect normalization, evaluator invocation, sealing.
rows.must_not_own: tmux mechanics or user-facing eval suite discovery.

rows.owns: ExecutionRunRequest/Response, exec command, tmux/harness/tool adapters, typed execution evidence.
rows.must_not_own: Eval policy, FlowMind authoring, semantic decisions.

rows.owns: Run/action records, leases, heartbeats, replay indexes, idempotency.
rows.must_not_own: Certification vocabulary or effect proof semantics.

rows.owns: Suites, cases, fixtures, claims, scorers, scorecards, discovery, reports.
rows.must_not_own: Runtime effect engine or general test classification.
rows.candidate_home: core/eval as projection over effect/evaluator substrate

rows.owns: Multi-agent handoff, group-chat, team allocation, shared context governance.
rows.must_not_own: Low-level graph traversal or effect receipts.
rows.candidate_home: Keep/rename core/orchestration only if this is its true scope

props.content:
FlowMind node / eval case / core gate
  -> binds vars + evaluator policy
  -> EffectEnvelope
  -> effect controller admits scope + authority
  -> core/exec executes the ACT through tmux/harness/tool adapter
  -> ObservedEffect is normalized from typed evidence
  -> evaluator programs score pass/fail/unclear + reasons
  -> EffectReceipt seals decision + typed proof refs
  -> projections render eval report, semantic-control route, receipt, dashboard

props.title: 1. Freeze the vocabulary
props.content: Write the boundary doc around existing primitives: `EffectEnvelope`, `ObservedEffect`, `EffectReceipt`, typed refs, exec request/response, run ledger. Avoid new type catalogs.

props.title: 2. Ban shadow execution
props.content: Inventory every `child_process`/shell site outside exec/harness/tmux-harness and route effectful work through `ExecutionRunRequest`.

props.title: 3. Split run ledger from proof
props.content: Keep RunService leases/idempotency; move certification semantics into effect receipt sealing with shared typed refs.

props.title: 4. Rename orchestration by scope
props.content: If it is multi-agent coordination, keep orchestration. If it is graph traversal, call it traversal/loop/runtime graph. If it is effect policy, put it under effect.
